description: A real-time collaborative editor backend, assembled from Asgard primitives — the same shape as a production collaborative editor. Provisions a Postgres metadata store, an S3 bucket for document blobs/snapshots, an HMAC signing key, an Auth0 SPA app, and a load-balanced HTTPS service. You bring the server image; Asgard wires the infrastructure. The agent fills the inputs and issues each request_resource itself.

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: OCI image for the collab server; reads DATABASE_URL_SECRET_ARN, DOCS_BUCKET, TOKEN_KEY_SECRET_ARN, AUTH0_CLIENT_ID, listens on 8080 with /healthz. Use immutable :sha tags.

inputs.name: subnet_ids
inputs.description: Two or more subnets across AZs.

inputs.name: certificate_arn
inputs.description: ACM cert for the app hostname; HTTPS is required for SPA login.

inputs.name: kms_arn
inputs.description: KMS key wrapping the secrets, for the task role's kms:Decrypt.

inputs.name: tenant
inputs.description: Auth0 tenant for the SPA app.

steps.description: 64-byte HMAC key for signing collab tokens.
The collab server behind an HTTPS load balancer.

after: Re-apply the spa auth0-application with callbacks set to the service url (see the 'wire-auth0-into-a-spa' guidance). Move real data only in a human-gated window.
